question_en: How many strings does a standard classical guitar have?
context_en: The Spanish classical guitar has 6 strings tuned E-A-D-G-B-E. Variants include 7- or 12-string guitars.

question_en: Who composed the Ninth Symphony with the 'Ode to Joy'?
context_en: Ludwig van Beethoven composed the Ninth (1824), his last symphony. He composed it when already completely deaf.

question_en: Which city are The Beatles from?
context_en: The Beatles formed in Liverpool in 1960. John Lennon, Paul McCartney, George Harrison and Ringo Starr.

question_en: Who is known as the 'King of Pop'?
context_en: Michael Jackson (1958-2009) received that title. His album 'Thriller' (1982) is still the best-selling in history (~70 M).

question_en: What is the typical instrument of Andrés Segovia?
context_en: Andrés Segovia (1893-1987) elevated the classical guitar to the status of a solo concert instrument.

question_en: Which Puerto Rican singer popularized 'Despacito' in 2017?
context_en: Luis Fonsi released 'Despacito' (with Daddy Yankee) in 2017. Its remix with Justin Bieber broke records on YouTube.

question_en: In which country was the tango invented?
context_en: The tango was born in the Río de la Plata (Buenos Aires and Montevideo) in the late 19th century. It is UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage.

question_en: Which famous composer was deaf at the end of his life?
context_en: Beethoven began to lose his hearing around age 26 and composed many of his masterpieces (including the Ninth) in total deafness.

question_en: How many notes does the diatonic scale have?
context_en: The diatonic scale has 7 notes: do, re, mi, fa, sol, la, si. Repeating, it returns to do for the octave.

question_en: Who was known as the 'King of Rock and Roll'?
context_en: Elvis Presley (1935-1977) popularized rock and roll in the 1950s. He sold more than 500 million records.

question_en: Which band wrote 'Bohemian Rhapsody'?
context_en: 'Bohemian Rhapsody' by Queen (1975), composed by Freddie Mercury. It lasts 5:55 and mixes ballad, opera and rock.

question_en: In which country was Plácido Domingo born?
context_en: Plácido Domingo was born in Madrid in 1941 and emigrated with his family to Mexico as a child. He is one of the Three Tenors.

question_en: How many keys does a standard piano have?
context_en: A standard piano has 88 keys: 52 white and 36 black, covering 7 octaves and a little more.

question_en: Who composed 'The Four Seasons'?
context_en: Antonio Vivaldi composed 'Le quattro stagioni' around 1720. It is one of the most popular Baroque works.

question_en: Which British singer died in 2011 at age 27?
context_en: Amy Winehouse (1983-2011) died at age 27 of alcohol poisoning. Her album 'Back to Black' won 5 Grammys.

question_en: Who composed the opera 'The Barber of Seville'?
context_en: Gioachino Rossini premiered 'The Barber of Seville' in 1816. It is one of the most performed comic operas.

question_en: What was Frédéric Chopin's nationality?
context_en: Chopin (1810-1849) was born in Poland and lived most of his life in France. He composed almost exclusively for piano.

question_en: Which band wrote 'Stairway to Heaven'?
context_en: Led Zeppelin recorded 'Stairway to Heaven' on their untitled 1971 album (Led Zeppelin IV). It is one of the most iconic rock songs.

question_en: Who composed the 'Bridal Chorus' (Wedding March)?
context_en: The famous 'Bridal Chorus' ('Here Comes the Bride') is by Richard Wagner, from his opera 'Lohengrin' (1850).

question_en: Which Mexican singer popularized the song 'Cielito Lindo'?
options_en: It's a folk song, no single artist
context_en: 'Cielito Lindo' is a Mexican folk song from 1882, composed by Quirino Mendoza y Cortés. Countless artists have performed it.

question_en: Which singer popularized 'Like a Virgin'?
context_en: Madonna released 'Like a Virgin' in 1984. The album sold more than 21 million copies and consolidated her career.

question_en: In which century did Johann Sebastian Bach live?
context_en: J.S. Bach (1685-1750), of the late Baroque. His works (BWV) add up to more than 1,100 pieces.

question_en: Which instrument does Yo-Yo Ma play?
context_en: Yo-Yo Ma is an American cellist of Chinese origin. He has won 19 Grammys and is one of the most influential living musicians.

question_en: How many members make up Coldplay?
context_en: Coldplay has 4 members: Chris Martin (vocals), Jonny Buckland (guitar), Guy Berryman (bass) and Will Champion (drums).

question_en: Which American singer composed 'Imagine'?
context_en: John Lennon composed and recorded 'Imagine' in 1971, during his solo career after the Beatles.

question_en: In which country did flamenco originate?
context_en: Flamenco originated in Andalusia (Spain) in the 18th century, with Roma, Andalusian and Arab influences. It is UNESCO heritage.

question_en: Which Spanish singer won Eurovision 1968?
context_en: Massiel won Eurovision 1968 with 'La, la, la', after Joan Manuel Serrat refused to sing it in Spanish.

question_en: Which musician is known as 'The Boss'?
context_en: Bruce Springsteen has had the nickname 'The Boss' since the 70s. His album 'Born in the USA' (1984) sold 30 M copies.

question_en: What is measured in hertz (Hz) in music?
context_en: Frequency is measured in Hz (cycles per second). Standard A is 440 Hz. Humans hear between ~20 Hz and ~20,000 Hz.

question_en: Which band recorded 'Dark Side of the Moon'?
context_en: 'The Dark Side of the Moon' by Pink Floyd (1973) spent 950 weeks on the Billboard 200 and sold ~45 M copies.

question_en: Who composed 'Swan Lake'?
context_en: Pyotr Ilyich Tchaikovsky premiered 'Swan Lake' in 1877. It is one of the most performed ballets.

question_en: How many members did the Beatles have?
context_en: The Beatles were 4: John Lennon, Paul McCartney, George Harrison and Ringo Starr (replacing Pete Best in 1962).

question_en: Which blues singer is nicknamed 'King of the Blues'?
context_en: B.B. King (1925-2015), a blues guitarist, named his Gibson guitar 'Lucille'. He influenced generations of musicians.

question_en: What type of instrument is the marimba?
context_en: The marimba is a percussion idiophone: tuned wooden bars struck with mallets. It originates in Central America/Africa.

question_en: Who composed 'La Traviata'?
context_en: Giuseppe Verdi premiered 'La Traviata' in 1853, based on 'The Lady of the Camellias' by Dumas fils.

question_en: Which Catalan singer-songwriter composed 'Mediterráneo' (1971)?
context_en: Joan Manuel Serrat released 'Mediterráneo' in 1971. It is considered one of the best Spanish-language songs of all time.

question_en: Which band released 'Hotel California' in 1976?
context_en: 'Hotel California' by the Eagles won the Grammy for Record of the Year in 1978 and is one of the anthems of classic rock.

question_en: Which orchestra did Herbert von Karajan conduct for decades?
context_en: Karajan conducted the Berlin Philharmonic between 1955 and 1989. He is one of the best-selling conductors in history.

question_en: Which is the highest note on the treble-clef staff?
context_en: In the treble clef, notes go up indefinitely with ledger lines above the staff. The highest note depends on the range.

question_en: Which British singer released 'Rolling in the Deep' in 2010?
context_en: Adele released 'Rolling in the Deep' in 2010, the first single from her album '21', one of the best-selling of the 21st century.

question_en: Who popularized the song 'My Way'?
context_en: 'My Way' is associated with Frank Sinatra (1969), although the lyrics are by Paul Anka, adapted from 'Comme d'habitude' by Claude François.

question_en: Which band recorded 'Smells Like Teen Spirit'?
context_en: Nirvana released 'Smells Like Teen Spirit' in 1991 on their album 'Nevermind'. It defined the grunge sound of the 90s.

question_en: How many lines does a staff have?
context_en: A staff has 5 lines and 4 spaces where the notes are placed. It is the standard Western system since the 17th century.

question_en: Which Mexican singer popularized the song 'La Llorona'?
context_en: Chavela Vargas (1919-2012) popularized 'La Llorona' in its best-known version, although the song is a 19th-century Mexican folk song.

question_en: In what year did Mozart die?
context_en: Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart died on 5 December 1791 at age 35, without completing his Requiem.

question_en: Which French musician inspired musical impressionism?
context_en: Claude Debussy (1862-1918) is the main exponent of musical impressionism, with works like 'Clair de Lune' and 'La mer'.

question_en: Which Puerto Rican singer is nicknamed 'El Conejo Malo' (The Bad Bunny)?
context_en: Bad Bunny (Benito Antonio Martínez Ocasio) is the most-streamed reggaeton/trap artist in the world on Spotify in several recent years.

question_en: Who composed 'Carmen'?
context_en: Georges Bizet premiered the opera 'Carmen' in 1875. It is one of the most performed operas in the repertoire.

question_en: Which band recorded 'Sweet Child O' Mine'?
context_en: 'Sweet Child O' Mine' by Guns N' Roses, on their debut album 'Appetite for Destruction' (1987). Slash's riff is one of the most famous.

question_en: How many operas did Mozart compose (approximately)?
context_en: Mozart composed about 22 operas, including 'The Marriage of Figaro' (1786), 'Don Giovanni' (1787) and 'The Magic Flute' (1791).

question_en: Who composed the 'Turkish March'?
context_en: The 'Turkish March' is the third movement of Mozart's Piano Sonata No. 11 in A major, K. 331.

question_en: How many symphonies did Beethoven compose?
context_en: Beethoven composed 9 symphonies. The 9th (1824) includes the famous 'Ode to Joy' in its fourth movement.

question_en: How many symphonies did Mozart compose?
context_en: Mozart composed 41 symphonies. The 40th and the 41st ('Jupiter') are the most famous.

question_en: How many symphonies did Haydn compose?
context_en: Joseph Haydn composed 104 symphonies. He is considered the 'father of the classical symphony'.

question_en: In which century did Mozart live?
context_en: Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart (1756-1791) lived in the 18th century. He was a central figure of musical Classicism.

question_en: Which country was Beethoven from?
context_en: Ludwig van Beethoven was born in Bonn (Germany) in 1770 and died in Vienna in 1827.

question_en: What nationality was Bach?
context_en: Johann Sebastian Bach (1685-1750) was German. He is a towering figure of musical Baroque.

question_en: Which composer wrote the 'Suites for Solo Cello'?
context_en: J.S. Bach composed six suites for solo cello (BWV 1007-1012), rediscovered and popularized by Pau Casals.

question_en: Which Catalan cellist revived Bach's suites?
context_en: Pau (Pablo) Casals rediscovered and popularized Bach's Suites for Solo Cello starting in 1890.

question_en: Which composer wrote 'The Messiah'?
context_en: George Frideric Handel composed 'The Messiah' in 1741. It includes the famous 'Hallelujah'.

question_en: Who composed 'The Nutcracker Suite'?
context_en: Pyotr Ilyich Tchaikovsky composed 'The Nutcracker' (1892), one of the most popular ballets in the world.

question_en: Who composed the '1812 Overture'?
context_en: Tchaikovsky's '1812 Overture' (1880) commemorates Napoleon's defeat in Russia and uses real cannons.

question_en: Who composed 'The Ring of the Nibelung'?
context_en: Richard Wagner composed 'The Ring of the Nibelung' (1848-1874), a monumental operatic tetralogy.

question_en: What nationality was Wagner?
context_en: Richard Wagner (1813-1883) was German. He revolutionized opera with his music dramas.

question_en: Who composed 'The Tetralogy'?
context_en: 'The Tetralogy' refers to Wagner's 'Ring of the Nibelung': 4 operas (The Rhinegold, The Valkyrie, Siegfried and Twilight of the Gods).

question_en: Who composed 'La Bohème'?
context_en: Giacomo Puccini composed 'La Bohème' (1896). Also 'Tosca', 'Madama Butterfly' and 'Turandot'.

question_en: Who composed 'Madame Butterfly'?
context_en: Giacomo Puccini composed 'Madama Butterfly', premiered at La Scala in Milan in 1904.

question_en: Who composed 'Aida'?
context_en: Giuseppe Verdi composed 'Aida' (1871), commissioned for the opening of the Suez Canal (although it premiered in Cairo in 1871).

question_en: Who composed 'Rigoletto'?
context_en: Giuseppe Verdi composed 'Rigoletto' (1851), based on a work by Victor Hugo.

question_en: How many operas did Verdi write (approx.)?
context_en: Giuseppe Verdi composed 28 operas throughout his life (1813-1901).

question_en: Who composed 'Don Giovanni'?
context_en: Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart composed 'Don Giovanni' in 1787, based on the myth of Don Juan.

question_en: Who composed 'The Marriage of Figaro'?
context_en: W.A. Mozart composed 'Le nozze di Figaro' (1786) to a libretto by Lorenzo Da Ponte.

question_en: What nationality was Chopin?
context_en: Frédéric Chopin (1810-1849) was Polish, although he lived most of his adult life in France.

question_en: Which instrument did Chopin master?
context_en: Chopin was one of the greatest pianists and piano composers in history.

question_en: Who composed the 'Boléro'?
context_en: Maurice Ravel composed the 'Boléro' in 1928. A single melody repeated with a crescendo orchestration.

question_en: What nationality was Debussy?
context_en: Claude Debussy (1862-1918) was French. The main exponent of musical impressionism.

question_en: Who composed 'Clair de Lune' (Suite Bergamasque)?
context_en: Claude Debussy composed 'Clair de Lune' as part of the Suite Bergamasque (published in 1905).

question_en: Who composed the 'Moonlight Sonata' (piano)?
context_en: Beethoven's 'Piano Sonata No. 14' is known as the 'Moonlight Sonata' (a posthumous nickname, not the composer's).

question_en: Which country was Antonio Vivaldi from?
context_en: Antonio Vivaldi (1678-1741) was Italian, born in Venice. A priest nicknamed 'il Prete Rosso'.

question_en: How many concertos did Vivaldi write (approx.)?
context_en: Vivaldi wrote more than 500 concertos. 'The Four Seasons' are the most famous.

question_en: Who composed 'Peter and the Wolf'?
context_en: Sergei Prokofiev composed 'Peter and the Wolf' in 1936. A musical tale where each character is an instrument.

question_en: Which country was Tchaikovsky from?
context_en: Pyotr Ilyich Tchaikovsky (1840-1893) was Russian. He composed operas, ballets, symphonies and concertos.

question_en: Which famous 'Chorus of the Hebrew Slaves' belongs to an opera by Verdi?
context_en: The chorus 'Va, pensiero' from Verdi's 'Nabucco' (1842), known as the 'Chorus of the Hebrew Slaves', became an Italian anthem of the Risorgimento.

question_en: What nationality was Sibelius?
context_en: Jean Sibelius (1865-1957) was Finnish. His work 'Finlandia' is a national symbol.

question_en: Who composed 'In the Steppes of Central Asia'?
context_en: Alexander Borodin, a physician-chemist and composer of 'The Five', composed 'In the Steppes of Central Asia' in 1880.

question_en: Who composed 'Pictures at an Exhibition'?
context_en: Modest Mussorgsky composed 'Pictures at an Exhibition' (1874) for piano; Ravel later made a celebrated orchestration.

question_en: Who is the composer of 'The Blue Danube'?
context_en: Johann Strauss II ('the Younger') composed 'An der schönen blauen Donau' in 1866. It is almost an informal anthem of Austria.

question_en: Which band recorded 'A Day in the Life'?
context_en: 'A Day in the Life' is the song that closes the album 'Sgt. Pepper's Lonely Hearts Club Band' (1967) by The Beatles.

question_en: In what year did The Beatles break up?
context_en: The Beatles announced their breakup in April 1970, after Paul McCartney's departure.

question_en: What was the last album recorded by the Beatles?
context_en: 'Abbey Road' (1969) was the last album recorded by the Beatles, although 'Let It Be' was released afterward.

question_en: Which is the best-selling album in history?
options_en: The Dark Side of the Moon
context_en: 'Thriller' (1982) by Michael Jackson has sold more than 70 million copies worldwide.

question_en: How many members did the Rolling Stones have (classic lineup)?
context_en: The classic Rolling Stones were 5: Mick Jagger, Keith Richards, Charlie Watts, Bill Wyman and Brian Jones (later Mick Taylor / Ronnie Wood).

question_en: Who is the singer of Queen?
context_en: Freddie Mercury (1946-1991) was the singer of Queen and the author of many of their biggest hits.

question_en: What was Queen's last concert with Freddie Mercury?
context_en: Queen's last concert with Mercury was on 9 August 1986 at Knebworth Park (UK).

question_en: Which band was David Gilmour the guitarist of?
context_en: David Gilmour joined Pink Floyd in 1968 replacing Syd Barrett and became their main vocalist and guitarist.

question_en: Which is Pink Floyd's best-selling album?
options_en: The Dark Side of the Moon
context_en: 'The Dark Side of the Moon' (1973) is one of the best-selling albums in history, with more than 45 million copies.

question_en: Who is the singer of U2?
context_en: Paul David Hewson 'Bono' has been the singer of U2 since its founding in 1976 in Dublin.

question_en: Which country is U2 from?
context_en: U2 formed in Dublin, Ireland, in 1976.

question_en: Which band was Kurt Cobain the singer of?
context_en: Kurt Cobain (1967-1994) was the singer and guitarist of Nirvana. He died at age 27.

question_en: In what year did Kurt Cobain die?
context_en: Kurt Cobain died in April 1994 in Seattle. He would join the '27 Club'.

question_en: Which artists died at age 27 in the so-called '27 Club'?
context_en: Hendrix, Joplin, Morrison, Cobain and Amy Winehouse all died at age 27.

question_en: Who composed 'Like a Rolling Stone'?
context_en: Bob Dylan recorded 'Like a Rolling Stone' in 1965. Nobel Prize in Literature 2016.

question_en: Who won the Nobel Prize in Literature in 2016?
context_en: Bob Dylan won the Nobel Prize in Literature 2016 'for having created new poetic expressions within the great American song tradition'.

question_en: How many strings does a violin have?
context_en: The violin has 4 strings tuned in fifths: G, D, A and E.

question_en: How many strings does a standard electric guitar have?
context_en: The standard electric guitar has 6 strings. There are 7-, 8- and 12-string variants.

question_en: Which instrument did Charlie Parker use?
context_en: Charlie 'Bird' Parker (1920-1955) played the alto saxophone and was a pioneer of bebop along with Dizzy Gillespie.

question_en: Which instrument did Louis Armstrong play?
context_en: Louis 'Satchmo' Armstrong (1901-1971) revolutionized jazz with his trumpet and his unmistakable voice.

question_en: Which instrument did Miles Davis play?
context_en: Miles Davis (1926-1991) played the trumpet. He is one of the most influential musicians in 20th-century jazz.

question_en: Which Miles Davis album is considered the best jazz album?
context_en: 'Kind of Blue' (1959) by Miles Davis is often considered the best jazz album in history.

question_en: Which singer popularized 'Strange Fruit'?
context_en: Billie Holiday popularized 'Strange Fruit' in 1939, a protest song about lynchings in the American South.

question_en: In which country did reggae originate?
context_en: Reggae originated in Jamaica in the late 1960s. Bob Marley is its most international figure.

question_en: In which country was Bob Marley born?
context_en: Bob Marley (1945-1981) was born in Nine Mile, Jamaica.

question_en: Which band was Bob Marley the leader of?
context_en: Bob Marley led 'The Wailers', first with Peter Tosh and Bunny Wailer, then just under his name.

question_en: Which singer is nicknamed the 'Queen of Soul'?
context_en: Aretha Franklin (1942-2018) is known as the 'Queen of Soul'. She sang 'Respect' and 'Natural Woman'.

question_en: Which band was Diana Ross the singer of?
context_en: Diana Ross was the lead voice of The Supremes (1964-1970) before her solo career.

question_en: Who is Stevie Wonder?
context_en: Stevie Wonder (1950-), blind since shortly after birth, is one of the greatest singer-songwriters and multi-instrumentalists of soul and funk.

question_en: Who composed 'I Want to Hold Your Hand'?
context_en: John Lennon and Paul McCartney composed 'I Want to Hold Your Hand' (1963). It was the Beatles' first No. 1 in the U.S.

question_en: How many albums did the Beatles officially release?
context_en: The Beatles released 13 official studio albums between 1963 and 1970.

question_en: Who is Joan Baez?
context_en: Joan Baez is an American folk singer-songwriter, an icon of 1960s protest and linked to Bob Dylan.

question_en: Who is Joaquín Sabina?
context_en: Joaquín Sabina (1949-) is one of the great Spanish singer-songwriters. Famous for songs like '19 días y 500 noches'.

question_en: Who composed 'Hijo de la Luna'?
context_en: 'Hijo de la luna' was composed by José María Cano for Mecano. It appears on the album 'Entre el cielo y el suelo' (1986).

question_en: Which Spanish band released 'Cruz de navajas'?
context_en: 'Cruz de navajas' belongs to Mecano's album 'Entre el cielo y el suelo' (1986).

question_en: Who composed 'Pongamos que hablo de Madrid'?
context_en: Joaquín Sabina composed 'Pongamos que hablo de Madrid' in 1980. Antonio Flores and Sabina have popularized it.

question_en: Who is the singer of Mecano?
context_en: Ana Torroja is the voice of Mecano, a band formed with the brothers Nacho and José María Cano.

question_en: Who composed 'El cant dels ocells' (a Catalan folk song)?
context_en: 'El cant dels ocells' is a Catalan Christmas folk song. Pau Casals popularized it as a piece for cello.

question_en: Which Latin act sold millions with 'Macarena'?
context_en: Los del Río (Antonio Romero and Rafael Ruiz) released 'Macarena' in 1993. Its 1996 remix was a worldwide hit.

question_en: Which country is the tango from?
context_en: The tango originated in the late 19th century in the Río de la Plata, in neighborhoods of Buenos Aires and Montevideo.

question_en: Who is Carlos Gardel?
context_en: Carlos Gardel (1890-1935) was the most famous tango singer. He died in a plane crash in Medellín.

question_en: Which singer popularized the song 'Volver'?
context_en: 'Volver' is a tango by Carlos Gardel (music) and Alfredo Le Pera (lyrics), from 1934. Pedro Almodóvar titled his film after it.

question_en: Who was Astor Piazzolla?
options_en: An Argentine composer of new tango
context_en: Astor Piazzolla (1921-1992) revolutionized tango by fusing it with jazz and classical music. He played the bandoneon.

question_en: Which characteristic instrument does the tango have?
context_en: The bandoneon, an instrument of German origin, is the characteristic sound of the Río de la Plata tango.

question_en: Which bandoneon player composed 'Libertango'?
context_en: Astor Piazzolla composed 'Libertango' in 1974. One of the most celebrated pieces of new tango.

question_en: Who composed 'Cinema Paradiso' (soundtrack)?
context_en: Ennio Morricone composed the soundtrack for 'Cinema Paradiso' (1988) along with his son Andrea.

question_en: Who composed the soundtrack for 'The Godfather'?
context_en: Nino Rota composed the soundtrack for 'The Godfather' (1972) and other Fellini films.

question_en: Who composed the soundtrack for 'Indiana Jones'?
context_en: John Williams composed the music for 'Indiana Jones', 'Star Wars', 'E.T.' and 'Jaws', among many others.

question_en: How many Oscars has John Williams won (as of 2024)?
context_en: John Williams has won 5 Oscars and has more than 50 nominations, an absolute record.

question_en: Who composed the soundtracks for 'Cinema Paradiso', 'The Mission' and 'The Untouchables'?
context_en: Ennio Morricone (1928-2020) composed more than 500 soundtracks. Honorary Oscar (2007) and Best Score for 'The Hateful Eight' (2016).

question_en: Which band recorded 'Wonderwall'?
context_en: 'Wonderwall' is by Oasis, from the album '(What's the Story) Morning Glory?' (1995).

question_en: Which country is the band Coldplay from?
context_en: Coldplay formed in London in 1996. Its members met at University College London.

question_en: Who is Chris Martin?
context_en: Chris Martin has been the singer and pianist of Coldplay since its founding.

question_en: Which band released 'OK Computer' in 1997?
context_en: Radiohead released 'OK Computer' in 1997, considered one of the best albums of the decade.

question_en: Who sings 'Smells Like Teen Spirit'?
context_en: 'Smells Like Teen Spirit' (1991) by Nirvana is one of the anthems of grunge.

question_en: In what year was Elvis Presley born?
context_en: Elvis Presley was born on 8 January 1935 in Tupelo (Mississippi).

question_en: In what year did Elvis Presley die?
context_en: Elvis Presley died on 16 August 1977 at his Graceland mansion (Memphis).

question_en: How many notes are there in an octave?
context_en: An octave has 7 natural notes (do, re, mi, fa, sol, la, si) but 12 semitones in total.

question_en: What musical interval is meant by an 'octave'?
options_en: The same note one octave higher
context_en: The octave is the interval between a note and the next one with the same name. Its frequency is double.

question_en: What is measured in BPM?
context_en: BPM (beats per minute) measure the tempo or musical speed: the number of beats per minute.

question_en: In which country was salsa (music) invented?
context_en: Salsa emerged in New York among the Caribbean communities (especially Cuban and Puerto Rican) in the 1960s-70s.

question_en: Who is Celia Cruz?
context_en: Celia Cruz (1925-2003), a Cuban, was the 'Queen of Salsa'. Her signature shout: '¡Azúcar!'.

question_en: Who is Rubén Blades?
options_en: A Panamanian salsa singer and politician
context_en: Rubén Blades is a Panamanian singer, composer, actor and politician. An icon of narrative salsa.

question_en: Which Spanish act performed 'Zorra' at Eurovision 2024?
context_en: The duo Nebulossa represented Spain at Eurovision 2024 with 'Zorra', although they did not win.

question_en: Who won Eurovision 2024 (Switzerland)?
context_en: Nemo won Eurovision 2024 for Switzerland with 'The Code'.
